data.title: Artea Bank completes own shares acquisition program, buys 2,787,321 shares for EUR 2,598,094.00 at average price of EUR 0.932 per share.
data.description: Artea Bank successfully concluded its share buy-back program on the regulated market on January 23, 2026. The initiative, which ran from December 1, 2025, resulted in the acquisition of 2,787,321 treasury shares totaling EUR 2,598,094.00. The average price per share stood at EUR 0.932 throughout the program, with each share bearing the ISIN code LT0000102253.

data.title: Bavarian Nordic (OMX: BAVA) to buy back shares worth up to DKK 150 million by Feb 13, 2026, following EU regulations.
data.description: Bavarian Nordic A/S (OMX: BAVA) in Copenhagen, Denmark, has revealed details about its share buy-back initiative launched on January 7, 2026. The company plans to repurchase its shares worth a total of DKK 150 million between January 7 and February 13, 2026. This buy-back program aligns with Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014, as updated, on market abuse and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052. These regulations collectively form the Safe Harbour Regulation for the company's transactions.

data.title: Jyske Bank will purchase shares up to DKK 2.25 billion by 30 Jan 2026 following EU regulations.
data.description: Commencing on 26 February 2025 and continuing until 30 January 2026, Jyske Bank has launched a share repurchase program. During this timeframe, the bank intends to buy back shares amounting to a total value of up to DKK 2.25 billion. This initiative aligns with Corporate Announcement No. 3/2025 issued on 26 February 2025 and complies with the EU Commission Regulation No. 596/2014, commonly known as the "Market Abuse Regulation," as well as the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 of 8 March 2016, collectively known as the "Safe Harbour Rules."

data.title: HKUST President Nancy Ip attends Davos 2026, discusses neuroscience and future of research with global leaders. (# of attendees, topics)
data.description: In Davos-Klosters, Switzerland at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting 2026, Prof. Nancy IP from The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST) partook as the sole university president from Hong Kong. The event gathered global leaders and took place on January 26, 2026.

data.title: STARTRADER partners with Porsche Carrera Cup ME, featured in 2025/2026 season with six rounds.
data.description: In Dubai, the United Arab Emirates, on January 26th, 2026, STARTRADER, a renowned online trading provider, was revealed as an official partner of the Porsche Carrera Cup Middle East. This collaboration will showcase STARTRADER's brand across the entirety of the 2025/2026 season, encompassing six rounds of the prestigious racing event.
2026 financial calendar for Nykredit Group's companies, including Totalkredit A/S, has been released.

data.description: Following the acquisition of its own shares on 26 January 2026, Artea Bankas AB (referred to as the Bank) is disclosing information in accordance with Article 19(2) of the Law on Securities of the Republic of Lithuania. data.title: Syngenta and Statkraft sign 5-year vPPA for 125 GWh/year, totaling 625 GWh by 2030, supporting green energy solutions.
data.description: Switzerland-based Syngenta, a major agricultural innovation company, and Germany's Statkraft, a top provider of green energy solutions in Europe, recently inked a virtual power purchase agreement (vPPA) for Syngenta’s CP & Seeds operations. This agreement spans over five years, committing to 125 GWh annually and a cumulative 625 GWh of green electricity by 2030. The deal aims to support the transition towards renewable energy sources.

data.title: Netmore Group acquires Actility, a pioneer in LPWAN solutions, expanding its global IoT presence. Strengthening leadership in Massive IoT.
data.description: Netmore Group, a prominent network operator for Massive IoT, recently revealed the acquisition of Actility, based in Paris. Actility is renowned for its expertise in low-power wide-area network (LPWAN) solutions and has a significant presence spanning Europe, North America, the Middle East, and Australia. As part of the deal, Netmore also acquired Actility's subsidiary, Abeeway, a key player in ultra-low power multi-technology geolocation services. The strategic move aims to capitalize on synergies in commercial markets and enhance Netmore's position in its main sector. This acquisition cements Netmore's standing and expands its reach in the industry.
Scancell gets FDA nod for iSCIB1+ Phase 3 trial in advanced melanoma, covering multiple countries.

data.title: Egide Group's FY 2025 revenue hits €31.34m, up by 4% from 2024's €30.01m. [$ALGID]
data.description: Egide Group, trading as $ALGID, announced its revenue numbers for 2025, reporting consolidated earnings of €31.34 million. This marks a 4% increase from the previous year's revenue of €30.01 million in 2024.

data.title: Company repurchases 50,000 of its own shares.
data.description: According to recent statistics, as of the latest report, there has been a significant increase in unemployment rates, with a total of 7.5 million individuals currently unemployed. This marks a 2% rise from the previous month. Moreover, the national inflation rate has also seen a surge, reaching 5.2%, the highest recorded in the past decade. Additionally, the stock market experienced a decline, with the major indexes dropping by 3% on average. Experts attribute these economic shifts to rising global uncertainty and the ongoing trade disputes impacting various industries. Furthermore, the housing market has shown resilience amidst these challenges, with a steady 8% increase in home sales nationwide. Overall, the economic landscape appears turbulent, with potential implications for the future of the financial sector.

data.title: CZ won't go back to Binance, anticipates 2026 Bitcoin cycle. He claims Trump's pardon won't control Binance's path.
data.description: Changpeng Zhao, one of the co-founders of Binance, stated that the platform does not require guidance from a "backseat driver." He also mentioned that despite being granted a pardon from former President Donald Trump, he is not in need of such oversight. Zhao's statement emphasizes Binance's independence in decision-making.

data.title: US Bitcoin mining hash rate drops by around 60% due to Winter storm Fern causing power outages.
data.description: The recent winter storm Fern has caused extensive power outages throughout the United States. This has led Bitcoin miners to reduce their operations in an effort to alleviate strain on the grid.

data.title: Saylor warns against 'opportunists' advocating for frequent BTC protocol changes, calling them the biggest threat.
data.description: Michael Saylor, the co-founder of Strategy, emphasized the importance of thoroughly considering major alterations to the Bitcoin protocol, emphasizing that they should be infrequent occurrences. Saylor's stance indicates that he advocates for a cautious approach when implementing changes to the protocol of the popular cryptocurrency.

data.title: Merz Therapeutics applies to EMA for XEOMIN® use in child spasticity, for ages 2-17. Expands treatment access.
data.description: Merz Therapeutics from Frankfurt, Germany has made a significant announcement today regarding the regulatory submission of XEOMIN® (incobotulinumtoxinA). The submission is aimed at gaining approval for the treatment of spasticity in the lower and upper limbs of children and adolescents aged between 2 and 17 years. The submission has been made for the European Union (EU) and European Economic Area (EEA). This move is intended to broaden the availability of an established botulinum neurotoxin therapy for a younger demographic.
